issues.note: Definition says 'interior point c is a critical point of f'. College Board's standard phrasing is 'c is in the domain of f'. The 'interior' phrasing is harmless here because endpoints are explicitly handled by the Candidates Test (slide 12), but a stricter wording would be 'c in the domain'.
Pause problem arithmetic verified. f(x)=x^3-12x: f'(x)=3x^2-12=3(x-2)(x+2), CPs x=±2. f(-2)=16, f(2)=-16. Sign chart +,-,+ confirms max at -2, min at 2. f''(2)=12>0 confirms min. All correct.
Both MVT hypotheses (continuous on [a,b] AND differentiable on (a,b)) explicitly stated. Conclusion formula correct.
EVT correctly requires continuity on closed bounded [a,b]. Counterexample f(x)=1/x on (0,1] is a valid 'drop closed-and-bounded' failure.
Inflection point definition correctly says 'where f'' changes sign' — not merely where f''=0. Avoids the x^4 trap.

States 'f''(c)=0 → INCONCLUSIVE' but does not SHOW WHY with the canonical pair: f(x)=x^4 (has a min at 0, f''(0)=0) vs f(x)=x^3 (no extremum at 0, f''(0)=0). A student adversary will say 'why is it inconclusive?' and the outline only asserts it. Adding both examples on this slide (or a small companion slide) would close the loop.

The 'f'(c) does not exist' branch of CP is mentioned in the definition but never demonstrated with a concrete corner example such as f(x)=|x| at 0 (where x=0 is a CP AND an extremum, yet the Second Derivative Test cannot apply because f' is not differentiable there). Students who only see f'(c)=0 examples will forget the DNE case on the MCQ. Worth one inline example phrase.
Geometric meaning of MVT (tangent parallel to secant) is explicit in both the visual and the example. Good.
Candidates Test step 2 explicitly says 'Add the endpoints', and ap_traps_covered #3 reinforces it. The FUN 5.5 endpoint trap is well-covered.
AP trap slide on f'(c)=0 not implying extremum is direct, with the x^3 counterexample. Good pedagogical pressure.

issues.claim: FUN 5.1 MVT, FUN 5.2 EVT and Critical Points, FUN 5.3 increasing/decreasing, FUN 5.4 First Derivative Test, FUN 5.5 Candidates Test, FUN 5.6 Concavity, FUN 5.7 Second Derivative Test
issues.note: Every FUN code cited in the outline (5.1 through 5.7) matches the reference text exactly. No code mismatches.
The outline does NOT repeat the '15-18% MC' weighting from the reference. Safer that way — no unsupported numeric claim to flag.
The '~15-16 class periods' figure from the reference is NOT repeated in the outline. Good.

issues.claim: No named researcher / no Cognia / no accreditation claim
issues.note: Outline is clean of unsupported attributions.

issues.claim: OpenStax Ch. 4.3-4.6 references and Khan Academy Unit 5 part 1
issues.note: These are external study-resource pointers in slide 17, not factual claims about AP content. Not citable against the reference excerpt and not flagged.
Slide 17 forward-references Module 6 covering FUN 5.8-5.12. This forecasts the next module, not a current claim; the reference excerpt only covers 5.1-5.7, so this is outside the citation scope.

decision_rationale: Zero critical issues and exactly one minor issue (Reviewer B: the Second Derivative Test slide asserts 'f''(c)=0 inconclusive' without showing the canonical x^4 vs x^3 contrast). All math is verified correct, every FUN code matches the reference, no unsupported numeric or named claims appear, and the AP traps (CP vs extremum, both MVT hypotheses, endpoints in Candidates Test, inflection requires sign change) are all directly covered. Under the rule '0 critical, <=1 minor -> SHIP', this module ships. The one minor gap can be patched in narration ('x^4 has f''(0)=0 yet is a minimum, while x^3 has f''(0)=0 yet has no extremum — that is why the test is inconclusive') without blocking production.
